description: Basic scientific facts about physics, chemistry, biology, and astronomy

facts.fact: Water freezes at 0 degrees Celsius (32 degrees Fahrenheit) at standard atmospheric pressure
facts.verification_source: International Association for the Properties of Water and Steam, NIST

facts.fact: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius (212 degrees Fahrenheit) at standard atmospheric pressure
facts.verification_source: International Association for the Properties of Water and Steam, NIST

facts.fact: The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792,458 meters per second
facts.verification_source: NIST, International Bureau of Weights and Measures

facts.fact: Earth's average distance from the Sun is approximately 149.6 million kilometers (93 million miles), known as one astronomical unit
Earth's circumference at the equator is approximately 40,075 kilometers (24,901 miles)
The acceleration due to gravity on Earth's surface is approximately 9.8 meters per second squared
facts.verification_source: NIST, International Bureau of Weights and Measures

facts.fact: Oxygen makes up approximately 21% of Earth's atmosphere by volume
Nitrogen makes up approximately 78% of Earth's atmosphere by volume
The average adult human body contains approximately 60% water by weight
facts.verification_source: National Academy of Sciences, Medical textbooks

facts.fact: DNA stands for deoxyribonucleic acid and contains the genetic instructions for all known living organisms
facts.verification_source: National Human Genome Research Institute, Encyclopedia Britannica

facts.fact: The Sun is a star located at the center of our solar system, approximately 4.6 billion years old
The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite, located approximately 384,400 kilometers (238,900 miles) away
The periodic table currently contains 118 confirmed chemical elements
facts.verification_source: International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC)

facts.fact: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert carbon dioxide and water into glucose using sunlight energy
Atoms consist of protons, neutrons, and electrons, with protons and neutrons in the nucleus and electrons orbiting around it
